name: Magdalena Gonzales Law Office
description_short: San Antonio-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and 13 filings, foreclosure prevention, and probate services. Operating since 1993 with bilingual staff.
description_long:
Magdalena Gonzales Law Office is an established bankruptcy and probate firm based in San Antonio, Texas, with nearly three decades of experience serving South Texas residents. The firm was founded in 1993 and has built a reputation for handling consumer bankruptcy cases and estate planning matters across multiple counties including Bandera, Del Rio, Eagle Pass, Kerrville, New Braunfels, Uvalde, and San Antonio.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy representation under both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 of the US Bankruptcy Code, along with probate and estate planning services. Their bankruptcy services specifically address creditor harassment and foreclosure prevention for individuals and small businesses. On the probate side, they provide will preparation, affidavits of heirship, medical powers of attorney, and durable powers of attorney to help clients protect their families and estates.

Key distinguishing factors include 29 years of demonstrated experience, bilingual (Spanish/English) staff capabilities, upfront and transparent pricing according to client reviews, and a commitment to creditor harassment prevention and foreclosure stops. The firm emphasizes personalized service with a small-firm approach focused on individual client needs. They offer free 60-minute consultations and accommodate both in-office and telephone meetings with clients.

The firm appears well-regarded based on available client testimonials, which consistently highlight professional communication, thorough explanations of legal processes, responsive customer service including after-hours text support, and reasonable pricing. However, as with any bankruptcy attorney, clients should understand that bankruptcy is a significant legal decision with long-term credit implications, and this firm's scope is limited to bankruptcy and probate matters only.
pros: 29 years of established experience in bankruptcy and probate law
Bilingual staff (Spanish and English) serving diverse South Texas communities
Free 60-minute initial consultation with no obligation
Transparent, upfront pricing with no hidden fees per client reviews
Offers both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy options
Specialized focus on foreclosure prevention and creditor harassment cessation
Available for both in-office and telephone consultations
cons: Limited to bankruptcy and probate services only—does not offer debt settlement, consolidation, or credit repair
Geographically focused on South Texas; unclear if they serve other states
No information about payment plans or financing options for legal fees
No online intake process or automated scheduling visible on website
Bankruptcy filings have significant long-term credit reporting consequences not prominently disclosed
best_for: Individuals facing foreclosure or aggressive creditor collection efforts
Spanish-speaking consumers in South Texas seeking bankruptcy counsel
Small business owners needing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 fresh start options
Estate planning clients seeking wills, powers of attorney, and probate administration
diagnosis: Magdalena Gonzales Law Office is best for South Texas residents needing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing or probate services, particularly those who prefer Spanish-language representation. The primary caveat is that bankruptcy is a major legal decision with lasting credit consequences—this firm handles the filing process but does not offer debt settlement, consolidation, or credit repair alternatives.

places_enrichment.adr_format_address: 2939 Mossrock, San Antonio, TX 78230-5142, USA
